title: No-Bake Protein Energy Balls
metaDescription: No-bake protein energy balls with 8g of protein each — just oats, peanut butter, and honey rolled into a grab-and-go snack. Ready in 15 minutes, no oven required.
targetKeywords: high protein snacks on the go
author: Recipe Team
keywords: protein energy balls, no bake protein bites, high protein snack

steps.name: Mix everything
steps.text: Combine oats, peanut butter, honey, protein powder, chia seeds, chocolate chips, and salt in a bowl. Stir until fully combined and slightly sticky.

steps.name: Chill the dough
steps.text: Refrigerate the mixture for 15-20 minutes — this makes it much easier to roll without sticking to your hands.

steps.name: Roll into balls
steps.text: Scoop about 1 tablespoon of mixture and roll into a ball. Repeat with remaining dough, about 12 balls total.

steps.name: Chill to set
steps.text: Refrigerate the rolled balls for at least 30 minutes before eating so they firm up.

tips.question: Can I make these without protein powder?
tips.answer: Yes — omit it and add 2 extra tablespoons of oats to keep the texture from getting too wet. Protein content will drop to about 4g per ball.

tips.question: How long do protein energy balls keep?
tips.answer: Up to 1 week in the fridge in an airtight container, or up to 3 months in the freezer.

tips.question: Why is my mixture too sticky to roll?
tips.answer: Chilling the dough before rolling is the fix — skipping that step is the most common reason people struggle. If it's still too sticky after chilling, work in an extra tablespoon of oats.

tips.question: Can I use almond butter instead of peanut butter?
tips.answer: Yes, any nut or seed butter works — just make sure it's a drippy, well-stirred consistency, not a dry or separated jar.
